name: Yemeni Ftoot

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Graham Crackers Flavored With Honey, Honey

steps: Tear the crackers into small pieces and toss with the melted butter.
Drizzle with honey and serve warm.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Graham Crackers Flavored With Honey, Honey

steps_detailed: Tear the crackers (standing in for torn flatbread) into small pieces and toss thoroughly with the melted butter and cinnamon.
Drizzle generously with honey and serve warm - ftoot, torn bread soaked in ghee and honey, is a simple, beloved Yemeni breakfast.

name: Yemeni Shakshuka
steps: Simmer the tomatoes and onion with the fenugreek for 10-12 minutes until thickened.
Make small wells in the sauce and crack the eggs into them.
Cover and cook 5-6 minutes until the eggs are set to your liking.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion, garlic, and jalapeno until softened, 5-6 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and fenugreek, and simmer 12-15 minutes until thickened into a rich sauce.
Make small wells and crack in the eggs, cover, and cook 5-8 minutes until set to your liking.
Yemeni shakshuka leans on fenugreek for its distinctive, slightly bitter depth.

name: Yemeni Qishr
steps: Simmer the ginger and cinnamon in 1 cup water for 8-10 minutes.
Sweeten with the honey and serve warm.
steps_detailed: Simmer the ginger, cinnamon, and fenugreek if using in 1.25 cups water for 10-12 minutes until fragrant.
Sweeten with the honey and serve warm - qishr, brewed traditionally from coffee husks with warm spices, is Yemen's signature morning drink, distinct from Arabic coffee elsewhere in the region.

name: Yemeni Fahsa

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef until very tender, then shred it finely.
Simmer the shredded beef with the tomatoes, fenugreek, and jalapeno for 15-18 minutes.
Serve bubbling hot with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef in water until completely tender, 1.5-2 hours, then shred it finely, reserving some broth.
Simmer the shredded beef with the tomatoes, fenugreek, jalapeno, and garlic, along with a splash of the reserved broth, for 18-22 minutes until thick and richly flavored.
Top with cilantro and serve bubbling hot, traditionally in the same clay pot it was cooked in, with flatbread for scooping - fahsa is a beloved Yemeni comfort dish.

name: Yemeni Shafut

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Mix the yogurt with the garlic.
Tear the flatbread into the yogurt and top with the diced cucumber.
Serve chilled.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Mix the yogurt with the garlic until smooth.
Tear the flatbread into small pieces and soak briefly in the yogurt.
Top with the diced cucumber and cilantro, and serve chilled - shafut, a cool yogurt and bread dish, is especially popular in Yemen's hot months.

name: Yemeni Fasolia
steps: Saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the beans and tomatoes with a splash of water, and simmer 15-18 minutes.
Serve with rice or flatbread.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic with the paprika until softened, 5-6 minutes.
Add the beans and diced tomatoes with a splash of water, and simmer 18-22 minutes until thickened.
Top with cilantro and serve with rice or flatbread - fasolia, a simple bean stew, is a common Yemeni home-cooked meal.

name: Yemeni Mandi
steps: Roast the chicken at 400F for 30-35 minutes.
Simmer the rice with the onion and cardamom in 1.5 cups water, covered, 20-22 minutes, and serve together.
steps_detailed: Season the chicken with half the cardamom and the cinnamon, and roast at 425F (220C), skin-side up on a rack over a pan, 30-35 minutes, until deeply browned, so the fat and juices drip down rather than staying with the meat.
Meanwhile, soften the onion in the oil in a separate pot, then add the remaining cardamom, cloves, saffron, and 1.75 cups water, and bring to a simmer.
Position the pot under the roasting rack for the last 20-25 minutes so the dripping juices fall into the liquid, then stir in the rice, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes.
Fluff the rice and serve the chicken on top.

Yemeni mandi shares the same drip-roasting logic as its Saudi counterpart - meat suspended above, rice cooked in what falls from it, traditionally in an underground pit here approximated with a rack over a pot.

name: Yemeni Saltah

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef with the potato and tomatoes for 1-1.25 hours until tender.
Whisk the fenugreek with a splash of water until frothy and foamy.
Top the hot stew with the fenugreek foam just before serving.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef with the potato, tomatoes, garlic, and jalapeno in a splash of water for 1.25-1.5 hours until completely tender.
Soak the fenugreek seeds in warm water 10 minutes, then whisk vigorously until it forms a thick, frothy foam.
Spoon the hot stew into a serving dish and top with the fenugreek foam just before bringing to the table, still bubbling.
Saltah is widely considered Yemen's national dish, its distinctive fenugreek foam topping unmistakable.

name: Yemeni Zurbian
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the rice, tomatoes, chili powder, and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken in the oil, 8-10 minutes, then set aside.
Deeply caramelize the onion and garlic in the same pot, 10-12 minutes, until a rich golden-brown.
Add the tomatoes, chili powder, cardamom, and cinnamon, and cook 3-4 minutes - zurbian carries noticeably more heat than Gulf-style machboos, closer to Yemen's spicier culinary character generally.
Return the chicken with 1.75 cups water and saffron, and simmer 20-25 minutes.
Lift out the chicken, stir the rice into the liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes.
Fluff the rice and top with the chicken.

name: Yemeni Haneeth
steps: Season the lamb with the fenugreek, garlic, and paprika.
Roast at 325F, covered, for 1-1.25 hours until very tender.
Serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Season the lamb thoroughly with the fenugreek, garlic, paprika, cumin, and oil.
Roast in a covered dish at 325F (165C) for 1.25-1.5 hours until deeply tender, basting occasionally.
Serve with rice - haneeth, slow-roasted meat traditionally cooked in an underground pit, is a Yemeni specialty for gatherings.

name: Yemeni Bamia (Okra Stew)
steps: Brown the beef with the onion and garlic.
Add the tomatoes and a splash of water, and simmer 20-25 minutes.
Serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, and cumin over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and 1/2 cup water, and simmer 22-28 minutes until thickened, finishing with the lime juice.
Serve with rice - a comforting, tomato-forward Yemeni stew, traditionally made with okra as its centerpiece vegetable.

name: Yemeni Malawah
steps: Make a soft dough from the flour, butter, and water. Rest 20 minutes.
Roll and fold repeatedly to create layers, then pan-fry until golden and flaky.
Drizzle with honey.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, butter, a pinch of salt, and water. Knead well and rest 25-30 minutes.
Roll the dough very thin, then fold repeatedly to create many thin layers, coiling into a flat round.
Pan-fry in the oil, 2-3 minutes per side, until golden and visibly flaky.
Drizzle with honey - malawah, a flaky layered flatbread, is a beloved Yemeni breakfast and teatime snack.

name: Yemeni Hummus

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth.
Serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, garlic, and oil with a splash of water until very smooth.
Serve with warm flatbread - hummus is common across Yemen, often served alongside other mezze.

name: Yemeni Spiced Roasted Nuts

ingredients.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps: Toss the nuts with the cinnamon.
Warm briefly in a dry skillet and serve.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps_detailed: Toss the nuts with the cinnamon and honey.
Warm in a dry skillet over low heat, 2-4 minutes, tossing frequently, until fragrant and lightly glazed.
Spiced nuts are a common accompaniment to qishr and coffee across Yemen.

name: Yemeni Sambosa
steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, cut into triangles, fill, and fold.
Fry until golden and crisp.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, cumin, and paprika.
Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin.
Cut into strips, fill with the beef mixture, and fold into triangles, sealing the edges well.
Fry until deeply golden and crisp, 4-5 minutes - sambosa, likely introduced through trade with South Asia, is a beloved Yemeni snack, especially during Ramadan.

name: Yemeni Bint al-Sahn
steps: Make a soft dough from the flour, a little of the butter, and water.
Roll into thin layers, brushing each with melted butter, and stack in a small pan.
Brush with egg wash and bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes until golden, then drizzle with honey.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, a little butter, and water. Divide into several portions.
Roll each portion thin and layer in a small buttered pan, brushing generously between each layer with melted butter.
Brush the top with the egg wash and bake at 375F (190C) for 22-28 minutes until deeply golden and flaky.
Drizzle warm with the honey and scatter with pistachios - bint al-sahn, a honey-soaked layered pastry, is Yemen's most celebrated dessert, traditionally served at weddings.

name: Yemeni Aseeda
steps: Whisk the flour into 1 cup boiling water gradually, stirring constantly until it forms a smooth, thick dough.
Spread into a bowl, make a well in the center, and fill with the honey and melted butter.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour gradually into 1.1 cups boiling water, stirring constantly and vigorously to avoid lumps, until it forms a smooth, thick, elastic dough, about 5-8 minutes of stirring.
Spread into a serving bowl, smoothing the top, and make a well in the center.
Fill the well with the honey mixed with cinnamon and the melted butter.
Aseeda, a simple wheat dough dessert eaten by dipping into the honey-butter well, is a Yemeni comfort tradition.

name: Yemeni Sabaya
steps: Make a soft dough from the flour and water, and roll into thin layers, brushing with melted butter.
Stack and pan-fry until golden and flaky.
Drizzle with honey and serve with cream.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour and water. Divide into portions and roll each very thin.
Brush each layer generously with melted butter and cinnamon, then stack and roll loosely into a coil.
Pan-fry over medium heat, pressing gently, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and visibly flaky.
Drizzle warm with the honey and serve with a dollop of cream - sabaya, a sweet layered bread, is a treasured Yemeni dessert.

name: Yemeni Hilbeh
steps: Soak the fenugreek in warm water 15 minutes, then whisk until frothy.
Stir in the cilantro, jalapeno, and lime juice.
Serve alongside saltah or grilled meats.
steps_detailed: Soak the fenugreek seeds in warm water for 15-20 minutes, then whisk vigorously until frothy and foam-like.
Stir in the cilantro, jalapeno, lime juice, and garlic.
Hilbeh, a foamy fenugreek relish, is a foundational Yemeni condiment, essential alongside saltah and many other dishes.

name: Yemeni Zhoug
steps: Blend the jalapeno, cilantro, garlic, and oil into a smooth, spicy paste.
Serve alongside grilled meats and flatbread.
steps_detailed: Blend the jalapeno, cilantro, garlic, oil, cumin, and lime juice into a smooth, vibrant, fiery paste.
Zhoug, a bright herb-and-chili sauce, is a Yemeni staple that has become popular across Israel and the wider region too.
